name: 0n1x
description: Neutral cryptographic trust layer for AI agents. Ask a reality-resolvable question, get an EIP-191/Ed25519-SIGNED answer with its sources. A signature proves the bytes were not altered — never that the claim is true.

skills.name: Verifiable Query
skills.description: Ask any reality-resolvable question. Returns signed observed facts with their sources. A signature proves the answer was not altered — never that it is true. Signs facts, never opinion or judgment.
examples: What is the price of BTC?
When was this domain first registered?

skills.name: Counterparty Facts
skills.description: Returns signed OBSERVED facts about a counterparty (registry status, domain age, TLS age, price observations) plus the sources they came from. Does NOT return a trust verdict, risk score, or recommendation — the caller decides.

skills.name: Merkle Census Proof
skills.description: Merkle-verifiable proof of the agent census, recomputable from public shards.

x-0n1x.note: Transport is a plain HTTP+JSON POST of our own shape — we do NOT claim A2A JSON-RPC binding conformance. Request/response documented below so a client is never misled.

response.proof: { id, alg: 'Ed25519', digest, signature, pubkey, issued } — present on the Pro tier

x-0n1x.verify: Ed25519 verify(proof.signature, proof.digest, proof.pubkey). The digest covers (message, reply, issued).

identity.note: Per-agent ProofCards are EIP-191 (personal_sign) over a passport statement; recover the signer and it MUST equal the agent's address. Answer signatures are Ed25519 (see proof.alg).
